title_en: Adh-Dhahabi's Critical Method and Hadith Evaluation
content_en:
Adh-Dhahabi's biographical work in Siyar A'lam an-Nubala is inseparable from his role as one of the greatest hadith critics of the Islamic tradition. His evaluations of the scholars he documented — their reliability as transmitters, their theological positions, their character — are delivered with the authority of someone who had devoted his life to mastering the hadith sciences.

His critical method combined breadth and precision: he had read widely across the entire hadith literature and the earlier biographical works, and he expressed his judgments with characteristic directness. He did not hesitate to note when a scholar's reliability was compromised by theological deviance, by lapses in memory in old age, by known dishonesty, or by excessive leniency in accepting weak narrations.

At the same time, adh-Dhahabi was known for fair-minded assessments that acknowledged the strengths of scholars he otherwise criticized. His entry on the Mu'tazili scholar and Quran commentator az-Zamakhshari, for instance, acknowledged his unparalleled Arabic linguistic genius while noting clearly his theological deviations. This combination of critical sharpness and intellectual fairness is characteristic of his best biographical work.

For scholars of his own era and those immediately preceding him, adh-Dhahabi's evaluations carry particular weight because he was in a position to gather information from multiple sources — written records, oral tradition, and personal acquaintance. His assessment of the reliability and significance of major Mamluk-era scholars has shaped the received judgments of later biographical literature.

His separate work Mizan al-I'tidal fi Naqd ar-Rijal focuses specifically on scholars who received negative evaluations in the rijal tradition, providing a comprehensive reference for assessing the reliability of transmitters who appear in hadith chains. Together with Siyar A'lam an-Nubala, it gives adh-Dhahabi a dominant position in the science of biographical criticism. Students who engage seriously with adh-Dhahabi's biographical corpus — reading Siyar A'lam an-Nubala for the full portraits of major scholars, consulting Mizan al-I'tidal for detailed narrator criticism, and using al-Kashif for quick reference assessments — gain access to the most comprehensive system of Islamic biographical scholarship available, a system built over a lifetime by the tradition's greatest master of the genre.
